tables.description: Stores medical certification records for crew members, tracking issue and expiry dates along with current status.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the medical certificate
Reference to the crew member holding the certificate
Date when the medical certificate was issued
Date when the medical certificate expires
Current status of the medical certificate

tables.description: Records duty periods for crew members, capturing start and end times linked to specific flights.

Reference to the crew member on duty
Timestamp when the duty period started
Timestamp when the duty period ended

tables.description: Logs compliance violations identified against crew members, including violation type and date.

Reference to the crew member who violated
Type or category of compliance violation

tables.description: System-level audit trail capturing user actions, affected tables, and timestamps for traceability.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the audit log entry
Reference to the user who performed the action
Name of the table affected by the action
Timestamp when the audited action occurred

tables.description: Tracks individual crew member enrollments in training sessions and their completion status.

Reference to the enrolled crew member

Reference to the crew member in the session

tables.description: Captures expense claims submitted by crew members with associated type, amount, and approval status.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the expense claim
Reference to the crew member submitting the claim

Monetary amount of the expense claim
Current status of the expense claim

tables.description: Stores notifications dispatched to crew members with title, message body, and creation timestamp.

columns.description: Reference to the recipient crew member
Short title or subject of the notification
Full text body of the notification
Timestamp when the notification was created

tables.description: Records mandatory rest periods for crew members to ensure regulatory compliance with duty time limits.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the rest period
Reference to the crew member resting
Timestamp when the rest period began
Timestamp when the rest period ended

tables.description: Assigns crew members to flights within specific rosters, including assigned role and current status.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the crew assignment
Reference to the assigned crew member
Role assigned to the crew member
Current status of the crew assignment

tables.description: Manages leave requests submitted by crew members, including leave type, dates, and approval status.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the leave request
Current status of the leave request

tables.description: Indicates crew member availability for specific dates with current availability status.

columns.description: Date the crew member is available

tables.description: Stores emergency contact details for crew members, including relationship and phone number.
Holds primary contact information for crew members such as phone, email, and address.
Maintains aircraft type ratings held by crew members with issue and expiry dates.

columns.description: Unique identifier for crew aircraft rating

tables.description: Records licenses held by crew members along with their issue and expiry dates.

columns.description: Unique identifier for crew license record

tables.description: Core master record containing employee details, rank, base assignment, hire date, and employment status.
Defines scheduled training sessions linked to a program, including date and instructor.
Catalog of training programs offered, including program name and validity period in months.
Reference list of license types available in the system.
Master data for flights including flight number, origin, destination, and scheduled departure/arrival times.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the flight record
Flight number code assigned to flight

tables.description: Defines monthly roster periods by month and year, indicating whether the roster has been published.

Flag indicating if roster is published

tables.description: Reference data describing aircraft types by code and name.
Reference list of available leave types.
Reference list of expense categories used in crew expense claims.
Reference data for crew base locations identified by airport code and base name.

columns.description: Airport code where crew base is located
Name of the crew base location

tables.description: Reference list of crew ranks recognized within the organization.

views.definition:
SELECT duty_log_id,
    crew_id,
    flight_id,
    duty_start,
    duty_end
   FROM dutylog;

entity_relationships.business_meaning: A Crew member is the central entity; they hold qualifications (licenses, ratings, medical certificates), are assigned to flights, have rest/availability records, enroll in training, may incur compliance violations, submit leave/expense claims, receive notifications, and belong to a rank and base (1:M to most, 1:1 to rank and base)
Flights are operated by AircraftTypes and assigned to Crew members through roster assignments; each flight has many crew assignments (M:M via crewassignment)

Crew members hold licenses (via crewlicense), aircraft type ratings (via crewaircraftrating linked to aircrafttype), and medical certificates; qualifications determine what crew can fly (1:M from crew)
Training programs contain sessions; crew enroll in sessions and complete simulator sessions on specific aircraft types to maintain/rank qualifications (1:M from crew to enrollments)
Crew members have rest period records and availability windows that determine flight eligibility and ensure regulatory compliance (1:M from crew)
Compliance violations and audit log entries track regulatory and policy breaches against crew members (1:M from crew)
Crew members submit leave requests classified by leave type; affects availability and roster planning (1:M from crew)
Crew members submit expense claims categorized by expense type for reimbursement (1:M from crew)
System notifications are dispatched to individual crew members regarding assignments, compliance, or training (1:M from crew)
AircraftType is a reference entity linking flights, crew ratings, and simulator sessions; defines equipment operated/flown
CrewRank is a reference lookup (Captain, First Officer, etc.) assigned to crew members (1:M from rank to crew)
CrewBase is a reference lookup for home airport/location assigned to crew members (1:M from base to crew)

business_processes.tables: license, aircrafttype, leavetype, expensetype, crewbase, crewrank

use_cases.name: Roster & Flight Assignment
use_cases.description: Build crew rosters by assigning qualified crew members to flights while respecting rest periods, availability, and leave.
use_cases.involved_tables: crew, crewassignment, flight, roster, availability, restperiod, leaverequest

use_cases.name: Qualification & Certification Tracking
use_cases.description: Monitor crew licenses, aircraft type ratings, and medical certificate validity to ensure flight-readiness and regulatory compliance.
use_cases.involved_tables: crew, crewlicense, license, crewaircraftrating, aircrafttype, medicalcertificate

use_cases.name: Training & Simulator Management
use_cases.description: Schedule training programs and simulator sessions, track crew enrollment and completion to renew or upgrade qualifications.
use_cases.involved_tables: crew, trainingprogram, trainingsession, trainingenrollment, simulatorsession, aircrafttype

use_cases.name: Compliance & Duty Time Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track flight duty logs, rest periods, and compliance violations to enforce aviation regulatory limits (e.g., FDP, flight hours).
use_cases.involved_tables: crew, dutylog, flight, restperiod, complianceviolation, auditlog

use_cases.name: Leave & Expense Administration
use_cases.description: Process crew leave requests and expense claims, integrating with roster planning to ensure adequate coverage.
use_cases.involved_tables: crew, leaverequest, leavetype, expenseclaim, expensetype, roster

sample_queries.question: List all active flight assignments for a given crew member including flight details
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT ca.assignment_id, f.flight_number, f.departure_date, f.origin, f.destination, a.type_name
FROM crewassignment ca
JOIN flight f ON ca.flight_id = f.flight_id
JOIN aircrafttype a ON f.aircraft_type_id = a.aircraft_type_id
JOIN crew c ON ca.crew_id = c.crew_id
WHERE c.crew_id = 101 AND ca.status = 'Confirmed'
ORDER BY f.departure_date;

sample_queries.question: Find crew members whose medical certificate expires within the next 30 days
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, mc.expiry_date, mc.certificate_type
FROM crew c
JOIN medicalcertificate mc ON c.crew_id = mc.crew_id
WHERE mc.expiry_date BETWEEN CURRENT_DATE AND CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '30 days'
ORDER BY mc.expiry_date;

sample_queries.question: Calculate total flight hours and duty hours per crew member for the current month
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT dl.flight_id) AS flights_flown,
       SUM(dl.duty_hours) AS total_duty_hours,
       SUM(dl.flight_hours) AS total_flight_hours
FROM crew c
LEFT JOIN dutylog dl ON c.crew_id = dl.crew_id
  AND dl.log_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
GROUP BY c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_flight_hours DESC;

sample_queries.question: Identify crew members with unresolved compliance violations in the last 90 days
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT c.crew_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, cv.violation_type, cv.violation_date, cv.severity
FROM crew c
JOIN complianceviolation cv ON c.crew_id = cv.crew_id
WHERE cv.status = 'Open'
  AND cv.violation_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days'
ORDER BY cv.severity DESC, cv.violation_date DESC;

sample_queries.question: Determine training completion rate by program
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT tp.program_name,
       COUNT(te.enrollment_id) AS total_enrollments,
       SUM(CASE WHEN te.status = 'Completed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS completed,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN te.status = 'Completed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(te.enrollment_id), 1) AS completion_pct
FROM trainingprogram tp
LEFT JOIN trainingsession ts ON tp.program_id = ts.program_id
LEFT JOIN trainingenrollment te ON ts.session_id = te.session_id
GROUP BY tp.program_id, tp.program_name
ORDER BY completion_pct DESC;

schema_patterns.description: Columns tracking creation and modification timestamps and users
